content: read reviews/293.md. if the summary section of the checklist review indicates the review passed, do nothing. if it failed, make relevant updates that are needed to deepmind/293.lean accordingly. if in doubt, reference CHECKLIST.md, which was the source document used when a previous instance of claude produced reviews/293.md.

file.content:
# Review: deepmind/293.lean

## 1. Copyright Header

- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line).
- [x] Year is exactly `2026`.

## 2. Import Statement

- [x] Exactly one import line, immediately after the copyright block (separated by one blank line): `import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports` at line 17.
- [x] No other imports are present.

## 3. Module Docstring

- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line (lines 19–41).
- [x] Starts with a heading: `# Erdős Problem 293`.
- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link to `erdosproblems.com/293`.
- [x] Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem (defining v(k) and asking about its growth).
- [x] Bibliography entries for related papers: [BlEr75], [vDTa25b], [ErGr80].

## 4. Namespace

- [x] All declarations are inside `namespace Erdos293 ... end Erdos293`.
- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase` (`Erdos293`).
- [x] `open` statements appear **before** the namespace block (line 43: `open BigOperators Real Filter Finset Classical`).

## 5. Custom Definitions

- [x] `AppearsInKTermEgyptian` (line 50) has a LaTeX docstring (lines 47–49).
- [x] `v` (line 56) has a LaTeX docstring (lines 54–55).
- [x] `v` is marked `noncomputable` (uses `sInf`, which requires classical choice).
- [x] No `sorry` in definitions.
- [x] `AppearsInKTermEgyptian` (a `Prop`) uses `UpperCamelCase`.
- [x] `v` (returning `ℕ`) uses `lowerCamelCase`.
- [x] No problem-specific notation needed; none introduced.
- [ ] No `@[category test]` or `@[category API]` sanity checks for definitions. *Optional — "consider adding" per checklist.*

## 6. Theorem Statements

- [x] All four declarations use the `theorem` keyword.
- [x] Main theorem is named `erdos_293` (line 94).
- [x] Names follow `snake_case` conventions: `erdos_293`, `erdos_293.variants.bleicher_erdos_lower`, `erdos_293.variants.van_doorn_tang_lower`, `erdos_293.variants.upper_bound`.
- [x] Each theorem has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` on the **same attribute line**.
- [x] All four theorems have docstrings.
- [x] Docstrings reference source papers: [BlEr75], [vDTa25b], [ErGr80].

### `answer()` usage

The original problem says "Estimate the growth of v(k)." The formalization interprets this as a specific conjecture — that v(k) grows at least doubly exponentially — which is a direct assertion (**Case 3**). No `answer()` wrapper is used, which is correct for Case 3.

- [x] Main theorem type is `P` directly (no `answer()` wrapper), consistent with a direct assertion/conjecture.
- [x] Quantifiers are not wrapped in `answer()` improperly. N/A (no `answer()` used).

## 7. Category Attribute

- [x] Every theorem has **exactly one** `@[category ...]`.
- [x] Values are valid: `research solved` for the three known-bound variants, `research open` for the main conjecture.
- [x] The `research open` theorem (`erdos_293`, line 97) contains `sorry` in its proof body.

## 8. AMS Attribute

- [x] Every theorem has **exactly one** `@[AMS ...]` attribute.
- [x] At least one AMS subject number listed (all use `11`).
- [x] Numbers in ascending order (single number, trivially satisfied).
- [x] No duplicate numbers.
- [x] `11` is a valid AMS MSC2020 code (Number Theory).

## 9. Proof Bodies

- [x] All proofs use `by\n  sorry` (multi-line form, acceptable per checklist).
- [x] No `sorry` in `def` or `abbrev` declarations.
- [x] No informal proof sketches for the open research problem.
- [x] No `native_decide`.

## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems

- [x] Variants are named `erdos_293.variants.<descriptor>`: `bleicher_erdos_lower`, `van_doorn_tang_lower`, `upper_bound`.
- [x] All variants live in the same file as the main theorem.
- [x] Each variant has its own `@[category ..., AMS ...]` attributes.
- [x] Variants are documented with docstrings explaining what bound they formalize.

## 11. Code Quality

- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
- [x] Unicode math symbols used: `∃`, `∀`, `∈`, `∧`, `¬`, `≤`, `ℕ`, `ℝ`, `ℚ`.
- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
- [x] No `set_option` that disables linters.
- [x] Lines are reasonably short (all under 100 characters).
- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.

## 12. Build Verification

- [ ] `lake build` — not verified in this review.
- [ ] All five custom linters pass — not verified in this review.

---

## Summary

**Result: PASS**

The file is well-structured and conforms to all checklist items. The formalization cleanly separates known bounds (as `research solved` variants) from the main open conjecture (doubly exponential growth). Definitions are appropriately marked `noncomputable`, all docstrings reference source papers, and the `answer()` usage (omitted, Case 3) is correct for a direct assertion/conjecture. The only non-checked items are the optional sanity-check attributes on definitions and build verification (not run).

content.text: The review **passed**. No changes needed to `deepmind/293.lean`.
